assertions.title: git log happy path — 10-commit log produces 10 oneline entries with >50% savings

steps.observed: Test passed — 14/14 tests pass in git-log.test.ts

evidence.testName: git-log filter > log produces oneline format
evidence.details: Test generates 10 verbose log entries, verifies filter produces 10 oneline entries each containing short hash and subject, and confirms filteredChars < rawChars * 0.5 (>50% savings)

assertions.title: git log truncation — 50 commits shows 20 entries plus truncation indicator

steps.observed: Test passed — 14/14 tests pass in git-log.test.ts

evidence.testName: git-log filter > log truncated at 20 commits
evidence.details: Test generates 50 verbose log entries, verifies exactly 20 commit lines shown and output matches /+\s*30\s*more/i truncation indicator

assertions.title: git log subject truncation — Subjects >80 chars truncated with ellipsis

steps.observed: Test passed — 14/14 tests pass in git-log.test.ts

evidence.details: Test creates 120-char subject, verifies subject portion ≤83 chars (80 + '...') and contains ellipsis. Additional tests confirm 80-char subjects not truncated and short subjects preserved.

assertions.title: git push/pull/fetch/add/commit single-line ok — Each action produces ok ✓ {summary} with >80% savings

steps.expected: Test 'each git action reduced to ok line' passes
steps.observed: Test passed — 18/18 tests pass in git-action.test.ts

evidence.testName: git-action filter > each git action type produces single-line output
evidence.details: Test verifies all 5 action types (push, pull, fetch, add, commit) each produce a single line matching /ok\s*✓/. Individual tests for push, pull, fetch, commit verify filteredChars < rawChars * 0.2 (>80% savings).

assertions.title: git push rejected preserves error — Rejected push preserves rejection reason

steps.observed: Test passed — 18/18 tests pass in git-action.test.ts

evidence.testName: git-action filter > rejected push preserves error
evidence.details: Test uses non-fast-forward rejection output, verifies 'rejected' is in output and result does NOT match /^ok\s*✓/. Additional test covers force-denied with protected branch hook.

assertions.title: git branch compact listing — Branch list is compact with current branch marked

steps.expected: Test 'branches listed compactly with * marker' passes
steps.observed: Test passed — 14/14 tests pass in git-branch.test.ts

evidence.testName: git-branch filter > branches listed compactly with * marker
evidence.details: Test provides 6 branches with main as current, verifies '*' marker present, 'main' present, and all other branches (develop, feature/auth, feature/dashboard, release/v1.0, staging) present in output.

assertions.title: git branch truncation — 50+ branches truncated with count

steps.observed: Test passed — 14/14 tests pass in git-branch.test.ts

evidence.details: Test generates 60 branches, verifies current branch present with '*', output matches /+\s*\d+\s*more/i truncation indicator, and branch count ≤50. Additional tests verify exactly 50 shows no truncation, 51 shows '+1 more', and current branch always visible even when beyond truncation limit.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 7 passed, 0 failed. All git-log (14 tests), git-action (18 tests), and git-branch (14 tests) passed successfully — 46 total tests green.
